description: MBP (E. coli K12 malE / MalE) — 396 aa maltose/maltodextrin-binding periplasmic protein precursor; canonical high-efficiency solubility-enhancing and amylose-affinity fusion partner

references.authors: Duplay P, Bedouelle H, Fowler A, Zabin I, Saurin W, Hofnung M
references.title: Sequences of the malE gene and of its product, the maltose-binding protein of Escherichia coli K12.

references.authors: di Guan C, Li P, Riggs PD, Inouye H
references.title: Vectors that facilitate the expression and purification of foreign peptides in Escherichia coli by fusion to maltose-binding protein.

references.authors: Maina CV, Riggs PD, Grandea AG, Slatko BE, Moran LS, Tagliamonte JA, McReynolds LA, Guan CD
references.title: An Escherichia coli vector to express and purify foreign proteins by fusion to and separation from maltose-binding protein.
Escherichia coli maltose-binding protein is uncommonly effective at promoting the solubility of polypeptides to which it is fused.
The ability to enhance the solubility of its fusion partners is an intrinsic property of maltose-binding protein but their folding is either spontaneous or chaperone-mediated.
Making the most of affinity tags.

references.authors: Sun P, Tropea JE, Waugh DS
references.title: Enhancing the solubility of recombinant proteins in Escherichia coli by using hexahistidine-tagged maltose-binding protein as a fusion partner.

references.authors: Momin AA, Hameed UFS, Arold ST
references.title: Passenger sequences can promote interlaced dimers in a common variant of the maltose-binding protein.

label: MBP
note: Maltose/maltodextrin-binding periplasmic protein precursor (MalE / MBP), E. coli K12 malE; UniProt P0AEX9, 396 aa. As stored this is the full PRECURSOR: an N-terminal 26-aa signal peptide (aa 1-26) followed by the 370-aa mature protein (aa 27-396). Periplasmic maltose/maltodextrin transport component; widely re-purposed as an N-terminal fusion partner that strongly enhances soluble expression of its passenger and binds amylose resin (eluted with maltose) for affinity purification.

provenance.sequence_source: UniProt:P0AEX9 (Maltose/maltodextrin-binding periplasmic protein, MALE_ECOLI, Escherichia coli K12; Swiss-Prot reviewed, SV=1, entry v183), 396 aa, 100% identity over full length, independently fetched and compared

functional_claims.label: MBP is an uncommonly effective solubility-enhancing fusion partner; the solubility enhancement is an intrinsic property of the MBP fold

value.mechanism: intrinsic to MBP structure; passenger folding may be spontaneous or require endogenous chaperones
value.comparison: more effective than GST and thioredoxin at promoting solubility of fused polypeptides

source.quote: Escherichia coli maltose-binding protein is uncommonly effective at promoting the solubility of polypeptides to which it is fused.

functional_claims.label: Solubility enhancement is an intrinsic property of MBP, but folding of the passenger is either spontaneous or chaperone-mediated

source.quote: The ability to enhance the solubility of its fusion partners is an intrinsic property of maltose-binding protein but their folding is either spontaneous or chaperone-mediated.

functional_claims.label: MBP binds amylose resin, enabling one-step affinity purification of fusions; bound protein is eluted with maltose

source.quote: Vectors that facilitate the expression and purification of foreign peptides in Escherichia coli by fusion to maltose-binding protein.

functional_claims.label: Native ligands are maltose and linear maltodextrins; ligand binding underlies the amylose-resin affinity handle

value.role: natural substrate of the periplasmic maltose/maltodextrin transport system; basis of the amylose-affinity purification step

source.quote: Sequences of the malE gene and of its product, the maltose-binding protein of Escherichia coli K12.

functional_claims.label: Tag is removed after purification by site-specific protease cleavage at a recognition site engineered into the linker (e.g. Factor Xa)

value.note: Cleavage site lies in the engineered linker between MBP and the passenger, not within MBP itself; the recognition sequence is supplied by the expression vector, not by this part

source.quote: An Escherichia coli vector to express and purify foreign proteins by fusion to and separation from maltose-binding protein.

functional_claims.label: MBP fusions accumulate to a high level in E. coli (on the order of a few percent of total cellular protein)

source.quote: expression of fusion protein to approximately 2% of total cellular protein

functional_claims.label: Native to and primarily used in Escherichia coli

value.origin: Escherichia coli K12 malE gene product

source.quote: the maltose-binding protein of Escherichia coli K12

functional_claims.label: Passenger sequences can promote interlaced (domain-swapped) dimers in a common MBP variant — a caution for structural studies of MBP fusions

value.context: observed in a common engineered MBP variant (MBPeng)
value.implication: can confound crystallographic / oligomeric-state interpretation of MBP-fusion structures

source.quote: Passenger sequences can promote interlaced dimers in a common variant of the maltose-binding protein.

functional_claims.label: A surface-entropy-reduced engineered variant (MBPeng) carrying ~8 substitutions is widely used to improve crystallizability of MBP fusions

value.rationale: surface-entropy reduction to aid crystallization of MBP-fusion proteins
value.relation_to_this_part: engineered derivative of E. coli malE / MBP; not the canonical P0AEX9 sequence

source.quote: a common variant of the maltose-binding protein
